articles.title: LBE Industry Pipeline Hits 16.5 Million Square Feet of New Development
articles.summary: JLL reports a massive 16.5 million square-foot development pipeline for location-based entertainment, signaling strong investor and developer confidence in the sector. For FEC operators, this represents both growing competition and validation of the asset class as a retail-adjacent draw. The scale of planned openings will reshape consumer expectations and local market dynamics across the country.

articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. This infusion of capital signals growing institutional interest in FEC-specific fintech and could accelerate product development for operators relying on the platform. FEC operators should watch how this investment shapes payment processing and cash management tools in the sector.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing to 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: ROLLER, a leading venue management platform for FECs and leisure businesses, has partnered with Adyen to provide fast, embedded financing through a new Capital product available to its 3,000-plus venue customers. This gives FEC operators streamlined access to growth capital directly within their existing software stack, reducing friction around funding expansions or equipment upgrades. The partnership positions ROLLER as a more comprehensive financial partner beyond ticketing and POS.

articles.title: ROLLER Launches Embedded Financing Solution Powered by Adyen
articles.summary: ROLLER's new embedded financing solution, built in partnership with Adyen, allows leisure and entertainment venues to access working capital through their existing platform. For FEC operators already using ROLLER, this means faster access to funds without navigating traditional lending channels. The move reflects a broader trend of vertical SaaS platforms expanding into financial services for their operator base.

articles.title: Semnox's Parafait Platform Powers New Romanian FEC with Cashless and Time-Based Play
articles.summary: Semnox Solutions has deployed its Parafait platform at Funzy Playground, a new indoor family entertainment venue in Romania, supporting 85 arcade games via RFID cashless readers and time-based billing for trampoline and play zones. The implementation also includes automated access control gates and real-time guest tracking via RFID wristbands. The case study highlights how integrated cashless and access-control technology can streamline operations for multi-zone FEC venues.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Opens New Location in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new venue in Carrollton, Georgia, adding another FEC to a market seeing continued growth in indoor entertainment options. New openings like this reflect the ongoing expansion of regional FEC chains into mid-size markets. Operators in similar markets should take note of Thunderzone's format and positioning as competitive intelligence.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Takes Over Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease for a portion of a former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, continuing the trend of FEC operators backfilling large-format retail vacancies. This adaptive reuse strategy has become a key growth avenue for the industry as landlords seek experiential tenants to drive foot traffic. FEC operators and investors eyeing mall-based expansion will find this a relevant case study in deal structuring.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Center in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new multi-level indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado, featuring a multi-level go-kart track as its signature attraction. The opening continues K1 Speed's national expansion strategy and raises the bar for go-kart facility design in the region. Competing FEC operators should take note of the multi-level format as a differentiator in dense urban and suburban markets.

articles.title: Spokane Valley FEC Closes Permanently After Two Years in Business
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley has shut down permanently after just two years of operation, underscoring the ongoing challenges facing independent FEC operators in competitive or under-trafficked markets. The closure comes ahead of the summer season, a typically critical revenue period for FECs. Industry stakeholders should examine the factors behind short-lived openings as a cautionary lens on site selection and capital planning.

articles.title: Supercharged FEC Concept Proposed Near Topgolf and Smash Park in Polaris, Ohio
articles.summary: A new FEC concept called Supercharged is being proposed for the Polaris entertainment district in Ohio, which already hosts Topgolf and Smash Park, creating a dense cluster of competitive social entertainment venues. The development reflects the growing trend of entertainment districts anchored by multiple LBE operators rather than single destination venues. FEC developers and operators should monitor how co-located entertainment districts affect individual venue performance.

articles.title: Cinemark's Gamescape FEC Concept Cancelled at Merriam Town Center
articles.summary: Cinemark's planned Gamescape entertainment center at Merriam Town Center in Kansas has been cancelled, marking a setback for the cinema chain's diversification into FEC-style venues. The cancellation may reflect broader challenges in executing theater-adjacent entertainment concepts in a shifting retail and entertainment landscape. Industry observers will be watching whether Cinemark pursues alternative sites or scales back its LBE ambitions.

articles.title: Intercard Showcases Award-Winning iQ QR Code Reader at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026
articles.summary: Intercard will exhibit its iQ reader, which supports QR code-based cashless gameplay, at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026 in Hong Kong from June 10-12. The iQ reader represents the growing shift in Asia-Pacific markets toward app-based and contactless payment systems for arcade and amusement venues. FEC operators sourcing cashless technology or eyeing Asia-Pacific expansion should engage with Intercard's team at the show.

articles.title: AAA Expo 2026 in Guangzhou Showcases Global Amusement and Attraction Innovation
articles.summary: The Asia Amusement and Attraction Expo 2026 in Guangzhou featured a wide range of new products across go-karts, bounce attractions, rides, and immersive entertainment technologies from manufacturers across China and beyond. The show serves as a key sourcing event for FEC operators and suppliers looking to identify emerging attraction formats and equipment suppliers. This firsthand report highlights the breadth of product innovation present at one of the world's largest amusement trade shows.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Fire-Breathing Dragon Centerpiece
articles.summary: A new resort destination called Dellshire has opened in Wisconsin Dells, anchored by a dramatic fire-breathing dragon as its signature experiential element. The opening adds to the competitive entertainment landscape in one of the Midwest's most active family tourism markets. FEC and resort operators should note the use of large-scale theatrical features as a strategy for differentiation and earned media.

articles.title: OKANA Resort Fully Open for Its First Summer Season in Oklahoma City
articles.summary: OKANA Resort in Oklahoma City is now fully operational for its inaugural summer season, offering a broad mix of water park, hotel, and entertainment amenities. The resort represents a major new entertainment destination for the region and signals continued investment in mixed-use leisure resorts outside major coastal markets. FEC operators in the Southwest and Southern Plains should watch OKANA as a benchmark for integrated resort-style FEC development.

articles.title: Mattel Wonder Indoor Waterpark Confirmed for North Carolina Resort Development
articles.summary: Mattel has announced that its fourth planned Mattel Wonder Indoor Waterpark will be part of the Weldon Mills Resort development in Roanoke Rapids, North Carolina, with an expected opening in 2029. The park will anchor a larger music- and entertainment-focused resort destination currently under development by Carida Capital Group. This expansion of branded indoor waterpark IP into secondary markets is a noteworthy trend for FEC developers and resort operators to watch.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Opens July 1 in Frisco, Texas — First Regional Park Designed for Young Families
articles.summary: Universal Kids Resort, the first theme park from Universal Destinations and Experiences designed exclusively for families with young children, will open on July 1 in Frisco, Texas. The 20-acre park features seven themed lands with IP from DreamWorks, Nickelodeon, Jurassic World, and Illumination. FEC operators targeting the family demographic should monitor this new benchmark in branded, kid-focused entertainment and its impact on regional family spending patterns.

articles.title: Play Playground Nashville Hosts Fourth of July Rooftop Fireworks Party
articles.summary: Play Playground Nashville, a social gaming and bar concept near downtown Broadway, is capitalizing on the Independence Day holiday with a rooftop fireworks viewing event featuring its 20-plus oversized games. The event strategy demonstrates how adult-oriented FEC concepts can drive incremental revenue by programming around holidays and local tourism peaks. Operators looking to maximize Q3 event revenue should take note of this example of experiential holiday programming.

articles.title: Fun Spot America Offers $28 Single-Day Passes for 28th Anniversary Promotion
articles.summary: Fun Spot America Theme Parks is running a birthday promotion offering single-day passes for just $28, representing a 59 percent discount with no blackout dates. The campaign is a classic anniversary-driven pricing promotion designed to drive summer attendance volume and reward loyal guests. FEC operators looking for promotional templates to drive summer traffic should consider milestone anniversary pricing as a proven tactic.

articles.title: London-Born Interactive Entertainment Venue Concept Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment space featuring virtual soccer, foosball, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is expanding from London to Paris, reflecting the international growth of social gaming venues. The concept blends sports entertainment with food and beverage in a format increasingly popular with young adult demographics. FEC operators developing or refreshing social entertainment concepts should monitor the European social gaming market for format and design inspiration.

articles.title: Six Flags Great America Revives Dual-Track Racing on American Eagle for 50th Anniversary
articles.summary: Six Flags Great America has announced that its iconic American Eagle wooden coaster will race side-by-side for the first time in nearly a decade as part of the park's 50th anniversary celebration. The move is a low-cost, high-impact way to refresh a legacy attraction and generate strong PR around a milestone year. It serves as a useful reminder for FEC operators that reopening or reactivating dormant experiences can be a compelling marketing and guest satisfaction tool.

articles.title: Gilroy Gardens Cherry Jubilee Returns with Food, Entertainment, and Family Activities
articles.summary: Gilroy Gardens Family Theme Park is hosting its Cherry Jubilee event from June 5 through July 5, featuring over 50 cherry-inspired food items, live entertainment, and family activities. Now in its second year, the event demonstrates how themed seasonal programming can extend visit duration and drive repeat attendance. FEC operators can draw inspiration from this food-forward event format as a strategy for building community loyalty during the summer peak season.

articles.title: Gateway Ticketing's Galaxy Platform Goes Live at John Ball Zoo in Comprehensive Campus Rollout
articles.summary: Gateway Ticketing Systems has completed a full campus-wide implementation of its Galaxy platform at John Ball Zoo in Michigan, integrating ticketing, retail, food and beverage, and revenue management into a single system. The deployment of the RevGen solution allows the zoo to streamline guest data across all touchpoints from arrival to departure. While zoo-focused, the platform capabilities are directly relevant to large FEC operators seeking unified guest management and revenue optimization tools.

articles.title: New Steel Coaster Patriot's Run Nears Opening at Jenkinson's Boardwalk
articles.summary: Jenkinson's Boardwalk in Point Pleasant Beach, New Jersey is conducting test runs on its new steel coaster, Patriot's Run, with an official opening announcement expected imminently. The addition signals continued investment in boardwalk-style FEC attractions on the East Coast. New ride openings at established venues are a proven driver of first-time visits and media coverage that FEC operators can replicate at their own scale.

articles.title: Moment Factory and Mitsui Fudosan Partner on Global Immersive Pokémon Experience
articles.summary: Moment Factory and Mitsui Fudosan have announced a multi-year partnership to create a touring immersive Pokémon experience, debuting in Europe in Spring 2027 and traveling worldwide with various operators and promoters. The project uses interactive technology and storytelling to let guests step into the role of a Pokémon Trainer in a live environment. For FEC operators interested in booking or hosting traveling IP-based immersive attractions, this partnership represents a significant upcoming opportunity.

articles.title: Hasbro Opens First-Ever Candy Land Café Immersive Experience in San Diego
articles.summary: Hasbro and Bucket Listers are launching a limited-time Candy Land Café pop-up in San Diego on July 9, featuring immersive themed environments, a branded menu, cookie decorating, and interactive activities based on the classic board game. The experience demonstrates growing demand for IP-driven, limited-run immersive dining concepts that attract families and nostalgia-seeking adults. FEC operators considering immersive pop-up or licensed experience formats can look to this launch as a model for engagement and monetization.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Lucknow, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has expanded its entertainment footprint with a new venue opening in Lucknow, signaling growth in India's indoor FEC market. The launch reflects broader international momentum in family entertainment center development across rapidly urbanizing markets in South Asia. Suppliers and franchise operators looking at international expansion should monitor India's growing appetite for structured indoor play and entertainment.

articles.title: Pacific Park Extends 30th Anniversary with Discounted Ride Offers Through June 11
articles.summary: Pacific Park on the Santa Monica Pier is offering discounted rides including $10 Pacific Wheel tickets as part of its extended 30th anniversary promotion running through June 11. The park's anniversary pricing strategy is designed to sustain celebratory momentum and attract value-conscious summer guests. FEC operators can take note of how legacy landmarks leverage milestone anniversaries to drive mid-week and shoulder-season attendance.

articles.title: Local Media IP Drives New Wave of Theme Attraction Development Across Asia
articles.summary: A comprehensive look at how locally produced media IP from gaming, anime, and streaming is fueling new location-based entertainment developments across Japan, China, and broader Asia. Deals between regional IP holders and attraction operators are reshaping what competitive entertainment looks like in the world's largest consumer markets. FEC operators and attraction designers should watch how IP-driven experiences gain traction beyond major theme parks and into smaller-format LBE venues globally.
